title: SketchTexts Object
description: The collection of text blocks in a sketch. This provides access to the existing text blocks and supports creating new text blocks.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: **RETIRED** Creates a SketchTextInput object that can be used to define additional settings when creating sketch text. The SketchTextInput object is equivalent to the Sketch Text dialog in that it collects all of the input required to create sketch text. Once the properties of the SketchTextInput object have been defined, use the add method to create the sketch text.

methods.name: createInput2
methods.description: **RETIRED** This method has been retired and replaced by createInput3. Use the new method to define text using an expression that can combine literal text with parameter values.

methods.name: createInput3
methods.description: Creates a SketchTextInput object that is used to define the additional input to create text. The SketchTextInput object is equivalent to the Sketch Text dialog in that it collects all of the input required to create sketch text. You must call setAsFitOnPath, setAsAlongPath, or setAsMultiLine methods to define one of the three types of text. Once the properties of the SketchTextInput object have been defined, pass the SketchTextInput to the add method to create the sketch text.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch text using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of texts in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Sketch Text API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating sketch text by creating both mult-line text and text along a curve.

add.title: SketchTexts.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchText object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SketchTextInput object created using the SketchTexts.createInput method.

related_samples.name: Sketch Text API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating sketch text by creating both mult-line text and text along a curve.

classType.title: SketchTexts.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SketchTexts.createInput Method
createInput.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Creates a SketchTextInput object that can be used to define additional settings when creating sketch text. The SketchTextInput object is equivalent to the Sketch Text dialog in that it collects all of the input required to create sketch text. Once the properties of the SketchTextInput object have been defined, use the add method to create the sketch text. Remarks
createInput.return_description: Returns a SketchTextInput object that can be used to set additional formatting and is used as input to the add method.

parameters.name: formattedText
parameters.description: The text used for the sketch text. This is a simple string as no additional formatting is currently supported.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the text in centimeters.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: The position of the text on the x-y plane of the sketch. The text must lie on the x-y plane so the Z component of the point is ignored and always treated as zero.

createInput2.title: SketchTexts.createInput2 Method
createInput2.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. This method has been retired and replaced by createInput3. Use the new method to define text using an expression that can combine literal text with parameter values. Remarks
createInput2.return_description: Returns a SketchTextInput object that can be used to set additional formatting and is used as input to the add method.

parameters.name: formattedText
parameters.description: The text used for the sketch text. This is the equivalent of the text that would be entered into the "Text" command dialog. It can be a simple string or it can be an expression that combines text with parameter values. In the case of a simple string, no quotes are needed, but for an expression, text should be quoted using a single quote.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the text in centimeters.

related_samples.name: Sketch Text API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating sketch text by creating both mult-line text and text along a curve.

createInput3.title: SketchTexts.createInput3 Method
createInput3.description: Creates a SketchTextInput object that is used to define the additional input to create text. The SketchTextInput object is equivalent to the Sketch Text dialog in that it collects all of the input required to create sketch text. You must call setAsFitOnPath, setAsAlongPath, or setAsMultiLine methods to define one of the three types of text. Once the properties of the SketchTextInput object have been defined, pass the SketchTextInput to the add method to create the sketch text.
createInput3.return_description: Returns a SketchTextInput object that can be used to set additional formatting and is used as input to the add method.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: This defines the expression of the parameter that will be created when this SketchText is created. It can be a simple string or it can be an expression that combines text with parameter values. Simple text must be enclosed within single quotes, the same as it is required in the TEXT command dialog.   An example of a valid expression is: "'Length: ' + lengthParam" and will result in "Length: 3.0 mm". The expression result can be obtained by using the text property on the created SketchTextInput object.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: A ValueInput that defines the height of the text. This value is used to create a parameter that will control the height of the text. It can be a value where it defines the height of the text in centimeters, or it can be a string where it defines the equation of the parameter and must evaluate to a valid length.

item.title: SketchTexts.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch text using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchTexts.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of texts in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchTexts_var.count

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
